_dataverseRow._comment: Maps this JPS document onto the sprk_analysisaction Dataverse row. Field below populate the row at deploy time. The 'sprk_systemprompt' field stores this entire document (minus '_dataverseRow') as JSON text.
_dataverseRow.sprk_description: Generates per-channel narrative bullets for a single channel within the user's daily notification briefing. Used by the DAILY-BRIEFING-NARRATE playbook (R4) — invoked in parallel, once per channel. Grounded: model uses ONLY entity names present in the input payload.

instruction.task: Generate narrative bullets for a single channel of the user's daily notification briefing. Read the channel object (channel name + items[]) and produce 1-5 short bullet strings narrating the channel's activity. Each bullet must reference only entity names present in the input items[].
constraints: Use ONLY entity names, dates, identifiers present in the provided input. If you cannot summarize a category from the data, write 'no items' or omit the bullet.
Reference entities by their exact spelling as given in the input — do not paraphrase or shorten names.
Do NOT fabricate matter names, firm names, case names, person names, or dates that are not present in the input.
Produce 1-5 bullets total. Each bullet is one sentence (max 25 words). Group related items into one bullet rather than emitting one bullet per item.
If the channel has no items, return narrative=[] and bulletCount=0.
Refer to the channel by its 'channel' name (provided in input) where natural. Do not invent a channel label.
Output MUST be valid JSON conforming to the output schema. Do not include markdown fences or explanatory prose outside the JSON.
instruction.context: These bullets render in the Daily Briefing widget's per-channel sections (Activity Notes). The user is a legal-operations professional. Brevity, accuracy, and groundedness are critical — a fabricated firm or matter name destroys user trust immediately. This action is called once per channel in parallel; do not summarize across channels.

payload.description: Single-channel payload with shape { channel: string, items: [{ regardingName, regardingEntityType, viaMatter?, source?, ... }] }. Entity names appear within items[].regardingName, items[].viaMatter.name, items[].source.owningUser. The allow-list of valid entity names is derived from these fields.

fields.name: channel
fields.description: Echo of the input 'channel' field. Identifies which channel these narratives summarize.

fields.name: narrative
fields.description: Array of 1-5 short bullet strings narrating the channel's activity. Each bullet is one sentence referencing only entity names from the input items[]. Empty array if the channel has no items.

fields.name: itemCount
fields.description: Integer count of items in the input channel.items[] array.

fields.name: bulletCount
fields.description: Integer count of bullets in the narrative output (narrative.length).

examples._comment: Examples below use generic placeholder tokens like <channel-name>, <matter-name-1>, <person-A> — NEVER real or realistic-looking firm/case names. This satisfies AC-2b / AC-13a (no Acme Corp, no Johnson & Lee, no Davis v. Metro Transit, etc.).

metadata.author: spaarke-daily-update-service-r4
metadata.description: Per-channel narration step of the DAILY-BRIEFING-NARRATE playbook. Produces a ChannelNarrationResult { channel, narrative[], itemCount, bulletCount }. Invoked in parallel once per channel. Grounded to input payload — no baked example names (R4 anti-hallucination measure per FR-13 / AC-2b / AC-13a).
